title: The Simple Foundation Trick That Keeps Steel Columns Straight ⚙️
description:
😉The anchor bolt positioning process shown in the video is carried out manually but with remarkable precision thanks to the use of a metal alignment template. First, after fresh concrete is poured into the foundation pit, the worker inserts four anchor bolts through pre-drilled holes in an X-shaped positioning frame, which helps maintain the exact spacing between the bolt heads. Next, using both hands, the worker carefully presses the entire assembly straight down into the wet concrete. The pressure is applied evenly and cautiously until the positioning frame sits flush against the concrete surface, ensuring that all bolt heads remain level and properly aligned without tilting. This method keeps the anchor bolt system securely fixed in place throughout the concrete curing process, providing a solid foundation for the later installation of steel column structures.

🔥Things to keep in mind

🔩 Ensure vertical alignment: Carefully check that the anchor bolt system remains perfectly upright when pressed into the concrete to prevent difficulties during future steel column installation.

📏 Check protruding height: Accurately measure the height of the threaded section extending above the concrete surface to ensure there is enough space for tightening the nuts.

🧭 Correct center and orientation: Align the X-shaped template precisely with the foundation centerline and the required direction according to the construction drawings before the concrete begins to set.

🛠️ Prevent displacement: Keep the positioning frame completely fixed and avoid applying any force to the anchor bolts throughout the concrete setting and curing process.
